steps.title: Identify the clause structures at the sentence boundary
steps.content: Left of the blank: an independent clause ending with “…has become an international.” Right of the blank: another independent clause beginning with “the Indian dance duo… defeated…”

steps.title: Apply the sentence separation rule
steps.content:
A period ends a sentence and begins a new one; a comma by itself cannot perform this function.

Choice D uses a period after “phenomenon” to properly separate the two sentences.

Choices A and B use only commas (creating splice errors), and choice C is ungrammatical.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
